failure_modes.text: Confirmation bias can turn evidence gathering into answer defense instead of genuine pressure-testing.
failure_modes.mitigation: Design falsifiable tests: before gathering evidence, specify what result would disprove the hypothesis, and commit to accepting that outcome.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring on the first hypothesis can block equal evaluation of alternative explanations.
failure_modes.mitigation: Generate competing hypotheses upfront: require at least two alternative explanations and evaluate evidence against all of them, not just the first.

failure_modes.text: Motivated reasoning can make the process look scientific while quietly steering evidence toward the desired conclusion.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate the evidence-gathering process from the hypothesis advocate: assign different people to collect data and to defend the preferred theory.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
What key outputs can I measure?
Is the worst case bad enough?

heuristics.text: Structure the work with a hypothesis pyramid so each sub-claim can be tested conclusively.
Do not boil the ocean; gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the hypothesis.
Use the dialectic standard so every thesis meets an antithesis before synthesis.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on disconfirming test structure, measurable outputs, and anti-answer-defense discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style research-process detail beyond the raw source's stronger hypothesis and evidence gating playbook.
open_questions: The failure-mode labels come from a later risk table rather than from the earlier anti-pattern block because they are more operationally precise.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems-thinking, design-thinking, and Feynman-technique as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is thinner than the explicit tension block, so most conflict preservation belongs in Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against confirmation-bias, status-quo-bias, and anchoring.
